steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two seaweeds. The notes show one specific difference: konbu's umami was rated moderate, while arame's was rated high. The correct answer must name both seaweeds and contrast their umami intensities.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — North Pacific konbu is a type of brown seaweed, but so is arame.: Points out a similarity (both are brown seaweeds), not a difference. ✗
  • Choice B — While North Pacific konbu and arame contain umami flavor, umami can also be found in tuna and garlic.: Shifts focus to tuna and garlic — irrelevant to a difference between the two seaweeds. ✗
  • Choice C — Although North Pacific konbu and arame are types of brown seaweed, the latter's umami flavor is more intense.: Concedes the similarity (both brown seaweeds) and then directly states the difference: arame's umami is more intense than konbu's. ✓
  • Choice D — Some types of brown seaweed, like North Pacific konbu and arame, trigger umami flavor in human taste buds.: Groups them together without contrasting their umami ratings. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
